skill.name: documentation-fundamentals
skill.description: Reviews READMEs, JSDoc, docstrings, and inline comments for clear purpose, audience fit, and useful context.
skill.summary: Helps improve developer documentation by emphasizing why decisions exist, not only what code does.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found no executable code or malicious behavior. The detections are false positives from markdown fences, documentation examples, and ordinary prose in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Interpreted as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are markdown code fences and documentation examples in SKILL.md. No Ruby backtick execution or shell invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is markdown guidance with fenced examples. No executable Ruby, shell wrapper, or dynamic command path exists in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Text Matched Weak Crypto Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections match words and example text, not cryptographic functions. No hashing, encryption, or security-sensitive algorithm is implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain documentation wording and examples only. I found no cryptographic API call, weak algorithm use, or credential handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Token Prose Interpreted as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detection is a comment example about refresh tokens in an authentication flow. It does not inspect the host system or collect environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is a block comment example that explains why refresh tokens avoid user disruption. No system command, file read, or host enumeration is present.

content.value_statement: Developer docs often explain what code already shows and miss the reasons behind decisions. This skill guides README, JSDoc, and comment reviews toward useful context.
actual_capabilities: Reviews README files for purpose, setup, quick start, and contribution guidance.
Evaluates inline comments for useful context instead of obvious code narration.
Guides JSDoc and docstring content for parameters, returns, examples, and errors.
Identifies outdated comments, magic numbers, commented code, and empty generated READMEs.
Provides Socratic review questions for mentoring documentation improvements.
limitations: Does not generate full product documentation from source code alone.
Does not validate that installation commands work in the current repository.
Does not replace project-specific style guides or API reference tools.
May need domain context to judge whether a comment explains the right reason.

use_cases.title: README Readiness Review
use_cases.description: Check whether a project README lets a new developer understand, install, and run the project.

use_cases.title: Code Review Comment Audit
use_cases.description: Find comments that repeat code and replace them with context about constraints, decisions, or edge cases.

use_cases.title: API Documentation Coaching
use_cases.description: Improve exported function documentation with parameters, returns, examples, and important failure behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Review a README
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of project onboarding documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this README using documentation fundamentals. Tell me what is missing, what is unclear, and what should change first.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Inline Comments
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of comments inside source files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these comments. Flag comments that explain only what the code does, and suggest comments that explain why decisions exist.

prompt_templates.title: Strengthen JSDoc
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of exported functions and methods.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this JSDoc for exported APIs. Check parameters, returns, examples, error behavior, and context a caller needs.

prompt_templates.title: Create Documentation Review Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a repository-wide documentation pass.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized documentation review plan for this repository. Focus on README gaps, API docs, stale comments, and maintenance risk.

output_examples.input: A README with only a project name and generated setup text.
output: Add a one-sentence project purpose near the top.
Explain why the project exists before listing setup steps.
Replace generated boilerplate with verified installation and quick start instructions.

output_examples.input: A comment says that code increments a counter before validation.
output: The comment repeats the code behavior.
Explain why the counter must change before validation.
Mention the edge case or business rule that makes the order important.

output_examples.input: A public function has a short description but no usage details.
output: Document required parameters and return meaning.
Describe errors or edge cases that callers must handle.

best_practices: Start documentation reviews by identifying the intended reader and their task.
Prefer comments that explain constraints, tradeoffs, edge cases, or business reasons.
Verify README setup and quick start steps against the current project.
anti_patterns: Adding comments that repeat names, assignments, loops, or other obvious code behavior.
Leaving generated README text that does not describe the actual project.
Keeping stale comments or commented-out code because deletion feels risky.

faq.question: What documentation does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews READMEs, JSDoc, docstrings, inline comments, API docs, and architecture decision records.

faq.question: Does it write complete documentation automatically?
faq.answer: It provides structure, review questions, and targeted improvements. Complete documentation still needs project-specific facts.

faq.question: Can it help with code comments?
faq.answer: Yes. It flags comments that repeat code and suggests comments that explain decisions or constraints.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed skill is markdown guidance and contains no executable code or malicious instructions.

faq.question: What is the main review principle?
faq.answer: The main principle is to explain why something exists, not only what the code already shows.
